name: WasmPlot Clean Makie API
description: Make the clean Makie-like WasmPlot API compile to WASM inside Therapy.jl @island effects

stories.title: Discovery: Dump and analyze IR for WasmPlot closure
stories.description: Run code_typed on the full WasmPlot closure (Figure + Axis + lines! + render!) and understand what the compiler produces. Identify which patterns (GlobalRef, invoke, foreigncall) appear and what fails.
stories.findings: 874-statement IR. All WasmPlot functions ARE inlined. Dict lookups for NAMED_COLORS produce jl_object_id foreigncalls. Kwarg wrappers (#lines!#3) appear as :invoke. Canvas/render functions appear as :invoke cross-calls.

stories.title: Fix kwarg wrapper auto-discovery for non-Base modules
stories.description: check_and_add_external_method! skipped kwarg wrappers (names starting with #) from non-exempt modules because they contain function singleton arg types. Fix: exempt kwarg wrappers from the function singleton check. Also refactor _autodiscover_closure_deps! to reuse check_and_add_external_method! instead of its ad-hoc Base-only filter.
stories.fix: Added _is_kwarg_wrapper check to exempt kwarg wrappers. Refactored _autodiscover_closure_deps! to call check_and_add_external_method!.

stories.title: Handle captured variable type instability (Main.sin ::Any)
stories.description: Captured variables from enclosing scope appear as GlobalRef with ::Any type, causing cascading type instability (Float64(freq_val) → ::Any, sin(x * f) → ::Any). These appear as :call (not :invoke) and the cross-call handler can't resolve them.
stories.findings: NOT A REAL ISSUE for Therapy.jl. The type instability only occurs with untyped Main globals (bare script variables). In Therapy.jl, signals provide typed values (CompilableSignal{Int64} → Int64), so sin(::Float64) resolves as :invoke with concrete CodeInstance. Verified: (1) typed let-closure produces zero ::Any :call statements, (2) typed closure compiles to valid WASM, (3) InteractivePlot island compiles to 95KB with no UNREACHABLE warnings.

stories.title: Eliminate Dict lookups in resolve_color (jl_object_id foreigncalls)
stories.description: resolve_color(:blue) inlines Dict.get() which produces jl_object_id foreigncalls — C runtime calls not available in WASM. Replace NAMED_COLORS Dict with if-else chain on Symbol comparison.
stories.fix: Replaced NAMED_COLORS Dict with if-else chain in resolve_color(::Symbol). Removed Dict entirely. Also: replaced COLOR_CYCLE Vector with if-else cycle_color(), added typed Vector{Float64} dispatch for lines!/scatter!/barplot!, fixed WasmTarget _apply_iterate isa(Tuple{}) check. IR: 874→252 statements, 4→0 foreigncalls. WASM island: 95KB→65KB.

stories.title: End-to-end WASM validation of InteractivePlot island
stories.description: Verify the compiled WASM actually runs in browser: canvas imports wired up, render! draws correctly, signal reactivity works.
stories.findings: WASM validates (WebAssembly.validate=true). Instantiation succeeds. Button handlers (_hw1, _hw2) work. Canvas imports match runtime (21 canvas2d functions). BUT _effect_0 traps with 'unreachable' before any canvas calls. Root cause: _render_axis! function in WasmTarget has codegen bug — standalone render! produces 'not enough arguments on stack for drop' validation error. Island compilation validates but hits stubbed code path at runtime. This is a WasmTarget codegen bug, not a WasmPlot API issue.
